description: Agent coordination manifest - claims prevent conflicts

agent-01-architecture.task: Architecture analysis and module boundary review

changes.issue: sha256 undefined name errors - imported inside functions but not at module level
changes.action: Added 'from hashlib import sha256' at module level and removed redundant local imports from log_session_start(), log_session_end(), log_policy_change()
Applied black formatting to fix formatting issues

architecture_findings: Server module (server.py) is the central orchestration hub - registers all tool servers and exposes ~60+ tool functions
Tool registration follows factory pattern: register_*_tools() functions create and register tools with the FastMCP instance
Agent system (agents/) is cleanly separated with BaseAgent abstract class and specialized sub-agents (GitAgent, SecurityAgent, etc.)
OrchestratorAgent coordinates sub-agents via TaskDispatcher with asyncio.gather for parallel execution
Permissions layer (permissions.py) provides agent-based tool permission matrix with runtime override capability
Skill system (skill_registry.py, skill_executor.py) has clear separation: registry manages metadata, executor runs skills in bounded subprocess
Detective system (_detective_*.py) handles error investigation with classifier/investigator/locator/learner/report sub-modules
Workflow engine (workflow_engine.py) implements state machine with explicit transitions and checkpoint support
No circular dependencies detected - imports flow cleanly from config/state -> tools -> servers
Consistent pattern: server.py uses lazy imports for heavy modules to avoid circular deps
boundary_observations: agents/ sub-module has clean boundaries: base.py defines interface, sub/ contains implementations, dispatcher handles coordination
skill_ modules (registry, executor, schema, builder, marketplace) form a coherent skill management layer
workflow_ modules (engine, agent_loop, context, project, runner, cache) handle workflow orchestration
_detective_ modules are internal helpers used only by detective.py - could be refactored as package
checkpoint.py delegates to snapshot.py - this indirection may be unnecessary but provides a stable interface
server.py is large (891 lines) - could benefit from splitting into tool-server registration modules

changes_proposed.issue: Turkish comment in docstring (Plan -> Onay -> Uygula -> Test -> Rapor)
changes_proposed.action: Fixed to English (Plan -> Approve -> Apply -> Test -> Report)

changes_proposed.issue: WorkflowEngine class docstring missing checkpoint and self-review details
changes_proposed.action: Added comprehensive docstring with state machine explanation

changes_proposed.issue: Pre-existing ruff errors (unused import, missing field import)
changes_proposed.action: Confirmed file already fixed by previous agent work; verified clean

changes: Move module-level imports (shlex, re) out of hot functions to reduce per-call overhead
Pre-compile regex patterns at module load time instead of per-function call
Remove redundant list() copies in cache returns
Optimize sensitive_path_reason_optimized with LRU-style caching for resolve().parts
performance_notes: Import inside function adds ~4% overhead per call - moved to module level
re.compile() in loops is expensive - patterns now pre-compiled at import
Cache returns use list(cached) unnecessarily - direct return saves allocation
Path.resolve().parts called frequently - added memoization for common paths

changes: Added _find_fork_bomb_unescaped() to catch fork bombs with escaped chars that bypass lowercase regex
Extended _DANGEROUS_ENV_VARS to include dyld_library_path (macOS DLL injection vector)
Enhanced _blocked_curl_wget() to detect silent fetch patterns and config/header injection
Fixed dangerous env var detection to trigger for PATH= anywhere in command, not just after env prefix
Added _blocked_sudo_n() to block sudo -n (non-interactive password attempt) and sudo -E
security_notes: Fork bomb bypass: original regex worked on lowercased normalized string, but \: \| \& escapes prevent lowercase from matching - now unescape before regex
macOS persistence: dyld_library_path mirrors ld_preload on Darwin for DYLD injection
Curl silently fetching remote content to shell is dangerous - added silent fetch detection
PATH= assignment anywhere in command can hijack command resolution

changes: Added session_start/session_end event logging with log_session_start(), log_session_end()
Added agent_id parameter to log_tool_call() for tracking which agent made the call
Added policy_change audit event type via log_policy_change()
Added ensure_session_start_logged() to auto-capture session start on first tool call
audit_improvements: Session lifecycle now has explicit audit events (session_start, session_end)
Agent attribution now possible via optional agent_id parameter
Policy changes (created, updated, deleted, enabled, disabled) are now auditable
